programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: Adopted Twin, Beaten, Strangled, Starved and Locked in a Room: The Exclusive Interview
programme.desc: Tina says her children lived in a house of horrors with their father and stepmother; two of the eldest speak out for the first time about the conditions they say they endured.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: Teacher Guilty of Sleeping With Student Is Now Suing Him!
programme.desc: Former teacher Tanya Ramirez, who had sex with a 17-year-old who attended the school where she taught, sues him and his mother, and they face off on stage.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: Schoolyard Setup Exclusive: Drugs Planted in a PTA Mom's Car
programme.desc: Former PTA president Kelli says that after a school spat, fellow parents framed her by stashing bags of drugs in her car; one of them, who has served time for her part in the incident, speaks out for the first time.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: The Girl in the Box: Kala Brown's Story of Being Abducted by a Suspected Serial Killer
programme.desc: Kala Brown, who made national headlines when she was discovered alive two months after she mysteriously disappeared in South Carolina, speaks out for the first time in an exclusive interview about her life in captivity.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: The Girl in the Box: Kala Brown's Story of Abduction by a Suspected Serial Killer, Her Dramatic Rescue
programme.desc: After spending more than two months locked in a metal storage container, Kala Brown continues to tell her story of captivity, her fight to survive and her dramatic rescue.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Primetime
programme.desc: Exploring grief: its unpredictability and the absence of a right way to process it; a young wife and mother diagnosed with ovarian cancer.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Primetime
programme.sub-title: 40 Years Behind Bars: Patty Prewitt Finally Free
programme.desc: Patty Prewitt is freed from prison, 40 years after being convicted of murdering her husband.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Primetime
programme.sub-title: Loving the Machine - When AI Becomes Human
programme.desc: As AI companions blur the line between connection and delusion, Dr. Phil investigates the rising trend of people turning to artificial intelligence for friendship, love and even conversations with the dead.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Primetime
programme.sub-title: Drinking and Driving: The Deadly Reality
programme.desc: With DUI fatalities on the rise, Dr. Phil speaks with a woman who went viral due to the body cam footage of her DUI arrest.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Primetime
programme.sub-title: Gone Without Saying Goodbye: The Truth About Suicide
programme.desc: Confronting an urgent and heartbreaking crisis; suicide knows no boundaries.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Primetime
programme.sub-title: Reading Between the Lines: The Battle for Literacy
programme.desc: Why are so many children struggling to read-and what can be done about it?

programme.title: Dr. Phil Primetime
programme.sub-title: Teacher's Mysterious Death: Was Ellen Greenberg Murdered?
programme.desc: On January 26, 2011, Ellen Greenberg was found dead with 20 stab wounds and bruises. Initially ruled a homicide, the cause of death was later changed to suicide. Her parents fought for justice, settling two civil cases in February 2025.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Primetime
programme.sub-title: Teacher's Mysterious Death: Was Ellen Greenberg Murdered? Pt. 2
programme.desc: (Part 2) On Jan 26, 2011, Ellen Greenberg was found dead with 20 stab wounds. Initially ruled homicide, it was later deemed suicide. Her parents settled two civil cases in Feb 2025. Now, the case is set to be reopened.

programme.title: Ancient Nutrition
programme.desc: Burning up to 15% more calories at rest by combining three ancient superfoods.

programme.title: Sleep Science
programme.desc: Two of the nation's sleep experts show you how to get a good night's sleep.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Wake Up
programme.sub-title: Arrested for Keying Her Ex-Boyfriend's Car
programme.desc: Autumn claims she was pushed to the point of insanity after being stuck in a cycle of abuse for almost two years, when, she says, her ex-boyfriend, D'Andre, choked her, head-butted her and sexually assaulted her.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Wake Up
programme.desc: Dr. Phil continues his conversation with D'Andre, who claims that his abusive relationship with his girlfriend, Autumn, is partly due to what he calls her "messed up" upbringing.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Wake Up
programme.sub-title: Our Lying Ex-Boyfriend Is the Next Dirty John
programme.desc: Tiffany's relationship with her child's father, Erik, was destroyed by his constant lying and empty promises; after doing research midway through their relationship, Sheka uncovered Erik's lies and put a stop to their wedding plans.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Wake Up
programme.sub-title: More Ex-Girlfriends Confront Accused Con Man Erik
programme.desc: Erik's ex-girlfriends Torri and Courtney and ex-wife Martha each have different stories to share about his deceitful behavior.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Wake Up
programme.sub-title: My Fiance Believes He's a Superhero
programme.desc: Kalan is desperate for Dr. Phil's help because her fiance has taken on the persona of a superhero and will answer only to "Goose Wayne Batman," refusing to take off his mask or eye makeup.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Wake Up
programme.desc: Nicole discovers that her husband of nearly 20 years has been having a sexual affair with a man; what a private investigator has found out about the other man.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Wake Up
programme.sub-title: Edge of Insanity: I Believe I Have a Second Schizophrenic Child
programme.desc: Susan and Michael, parents of schizophrenic Janni, return to the show at odds over their 11-year-old son, Bodhi, who Susan is convinced is schizophrenic as well; Michael thinks Susan's quest for a diagnosis will harm their son.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Wake Up
programme.sub-title: A Second Schizophrenic Child or a Misdiagnosing Mom: Can Bodhi Be Helped?
programme.desc: Dr. Phil continues his discussion with exes Michael and Susan about the care of 11-year-old Bodhi, who Susan insists is dealing with schizophrenia, just like their 16-year-old daughter, Janni.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: Caught on Tape: My Married Dentist Lover Accused of Murdering My Son
programme.desc: Authorities say Roxanne's boyfriend was caught on home-surveillance cameras causing life-ending injuries to her baby son; she speaks out.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: Mother Knows Best: The Story of Gypsy Rose
programme.desc: Gypsy Rose Blanchard's mother forces her to pretend to be sick, makes her undergo countless surgeries, shaves her head, has a feeding tube inserted and makes her use a wheelchair; the deceit and torture eventually breed a contempt leading to murder.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: Gypsy Rose: From Wheelchair Bound to Behind Bars for Murder
programme.desc: A severe case of Munchausen syndrome by proxy leads to murder: Gypsy Rose Blanchard decides that killing her own mother is her only way to escape years of abuse.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: Exclusive: Grieving Parents React to Chris Watts Confession
programme.desc: Authorities release a confession from killer dad Chris Watts describing the murder of his pregnant wife, Shan'ann, and their two daughters; in an exclusive interview, Shan'ann's parents and brother reveal their reactions to learning the details.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: Ukrainian Orphan: Child or Adult Sociopath?
programme.desc: An interview with the Ukrainian orphan whose adoptive parents have been accused of abandoning her when she was just 8 years old; they say Natalia, who has a form of dwarfism, is really a sociopathic adult posing as a child -- who tried to kill them.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: My Sister Is a Cult Leader Who Claims She's Mother God, Part 1
programme.desc: Chelsea says her sister Amy left the family, started calling herself Mother God, claims to do surgery with her hands and talk to dead people.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: Mother God's Followers and Alleged Victims Face Off
programme.desc: Amy says she is "Mother God" and can heal the world with her group, Love Has Won; sister Chelsea states that Amy is a brainwashed "cult leader" who abandoned her family years ago and is putting other people's lives at risk.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Primetime
programme.sub-title: The Hidden Reality of Human Trafficking
programme.desc: It's a multi-billion-dollar industry built on manipulation, coercion, and unimaginable suffering; human trafficking and it's happening right here in the United States.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Primetime
programme.desc: Donald Trump has put many Americans' frustration with DEI front and center; Dr. Phil interviews those who feel they've been bullied or lost jobs because of DEI or reverse discrimination.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Primetime
programme.sub-title: When Words Become Crimes: The Cost of School Shooting Threats
programme.desc: How should we handle school threats and how far should law enforcement go to stop the next tragedy?

programme.title: Dr. Phil Primetime
programme.sub-title: Breaking Point: America's Rage Against Health Insurance Companies
programme.desc: The murder of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson reveals two aspects of American life; a health care executive was shot dead and because he was a health care executive, people cheered.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Primetime
programme.sub-title: American Wombs for Sale: The Rise of Chinese Birth Tourism
programme.desc: Is American citizenship quietly becoming a commodity and who's cashing in?

programme.title: Dr. Phil Primetime
programme.sub-title: The Workplace Debate: Work From Home or Return to Office
programme.desc: The debate on whether employees should be able to work from home or be mandated back to the office.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Primetime
programme.desc: Dr. Phil confronts the harrowing reality of individuals whose lives are shattered in an instant by unexpected acts of brutality they never saw coming.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Primetime
programme.sub-title: The Lost Boys: A Crisis in Masculinity
programme.desc: Why are so many boys falling behind and what's driving them into the darkest corners of the internet?

programme.title: Sleep Science
programme.desc: Two of the nation's sleep experts show you how to get a good night's sleep.

programme.title: Ancient Nutrition
programme.desc: Not drinking enough water every day? We found a new product that will keep you hydrated all day; hosts Sonia Azad and Jordan Rubin show us the secret to rapid hydration.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Wake Up
programme.sub-title: Edge of Insanity: I Believe I Have a Second Schizophrenic Child
programme.desc: Susan and Michael, parents of schizophrenic Janni, return to the show at odds over their 11-year-old son, Bodhi, who Susan is convinced is schizophrenic as well; Michael thinks Susan's quest for a diagnosis will harm their son.

programme.title: Dr. Phil Wake Up
programme.sub-title: A Second Schizophrenic Child or a Misdiagnosing Mom: Can Bodhi Be Helped?
programme.desc: Dr. Phil continues his discussion with exes Michael and Susan about the care of 11-year-old Bodhi, who Susan insists is dealing with schizophrenia, just like their 16-year-old daughter, Janni.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: Actress Accused: Was She an Accomplice to Murder?
programme.desc: A woman whose fiance is behind bars awaiting trial in a double homicide says she is innocent of helping to cover up the crimes; a victim's father and a district attorney weigh in.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: Baby Snatcher: Barely Escaping a Fetal Abduction?
programme.desc: A young woman breaks her silence about a sinister plot she says nearly claimed her life and that of her unborn baby; a woman is obsessed with becoming a mother and has resorted to drastic measures to get pregnant -- without her husband's consent.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: The Boy in the Bunker: The Exclusive Interview With Ethan and His Mother
programme.desc: Six-year-old Ethan, who was abducted at gunpoint and held captive in an underground bunker in Alabama for nearly a week, and his mother, Jennifer, talk to Dr. Phil; specialists tell how to help him cope with what he has experienced.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: Are My Children in a Cult?
programme.desc: James accuses his estranged wife of being controlled by a religious group and says he fears that his four children may be in danger.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.sub-title: Exclusive: Ariel Castro's First Victim Speaks Out
programme.desc: Ariel Castro abduction survivor Michelle Knight reveals the acts of cruelty that took place in the Cleveland house.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: Chained, Tortured and Starved: The First Cleveland Kidnapping Victim Reveals All
programme.desc: Ariel Castro abduction survivor Michelle Knight reveals details of more than a decade of captivity in the Cleveland house, the day she and the other women were rescued and what her life has been like since.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.desc: The birth mother and stepmother of a 5-year-old boy said to have been locked in a closet and starved nearly to death tell their sides of the story.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.desc: The birth mother and stepmother of a 5-year-old boy said to have been locked in a closet and starved nearly to death tell their sides of the story.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: Vanished on Vacation: Beautiful Blonde Goes Missing in Aruba
programme.desc: Dr. Phil sits down with Gary Giordano, who was considered the prime suspect in the disappearance of traveling companion Robyn Gardner in 2011 Aruba, and offers him a polygraph test.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: The Doctor, His Wife, His Mistress, the Murder
programme.desc: Gypsy Willis, the former mistress of a doctor recently convicted in the 2007 murder of his wife, discusses the case and her own 2009 conviction of fraud.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: Love, Lies and Murder: Killer Doctor's Daughter Reacts to Mistress Interview
programme.desc: A daughter of Dr. Martin MacNeill tells why she helped prosecutors build a case against him in the death of her mother and reacts to Dr. Phil's interview with her father's former mistress, Gypsy Willis.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: A Family Slaughtered for Teen Love: The Convicted Daughter Speaks Out
programme.desc: Erin Caffey speaks out from prison about the night her boyfriend and two accomplices brutally killed her mother and younger brothers and shot her father, who also discusses the ordeal.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: My Family Slaughtered for My Daughter's Teenage Love: Can a Father Forgive?
programme.desc: Erin Caffey discusses her role in the brutal murders of her mother and younger brothers and how she felt when she learned that her father survived; her ex-boyfriend makes a shocking claim.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: Mysteriously, Their Children Died Three Months Apart: Dad Thinks Mom Is to Blame
programme.desc: Julia and Codey vehemently deny any wrongdoing in the mysterious deaths of their 1-year-old son and 4-year-old daughter three months apart.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: Parents Accused of Murder: Will Julia and Codey Take the Polygraph Test?
programme.desc: Parents of two toddlers who died mysteriously, three months apart, are offered the opportunity to take lie-detector tests to clear their names.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: The Night Four Girls Went Into the Woods and Only Three Came Out Alive
programme.desc: A 17-year-old girl is sentenced to 23 years behind bars for luring a friend into the woods and killing her; Shemida, whose family accuses her of fabricating health problems for attention, returns to the show.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: Exclusive: The Vanderbilt Football Player's Interview From Behind Bars
programme.desc: One of two former Vanderbilt University football players who were found guilty of raping an unconscious female classmate in a campus dorm room after a night of drinking tells his story.

programme.title: Dr. Phil: True Crime
programme.sub-title: The Student and the Professor: Is One Stalking the Other?
programme.desc: Dr. Phil tries to unravel the truth about a toxic relationship between a professor and one of her students.

programme.title: Ancient Skies

programme.title: Ancient Skies
programme.desc: Moments in history that reshape the solar system and provide an understanding of the universe.

programme.title: Iconic America: Our Symbols and Stories With David Rubenstein
programme.desc: A real estate advertisement erected in 1923 becomes an international symbol of fame, fortune and the American dream.

programme.title: Iconic America: Our Symbols and Stories With David Rubenstein
programme.desc: How the "Don't Tread on Me" flag has been co-opted by a wide variety of Americans over its long history.

programme.title: Iconic America: Our Symbols and Stories With David Rubenstein
programme.desc: The myths and realities of the American cowboy.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.sub-title: He Said, He Said; Bunny Trouble
programme.desc: When an episode of Bionic Bunny is interrupted, Carl is the only one who can recall what comes next; Larry, the class bunny, wanders off.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.sub-title: Dance Like Nobody Is Watching; Recipe for Disaster
programme.desc: Olive, Otto, Ms. O and Oscar navigate booby traps when trying to silence an alarm system; Olive and Otto have their own explanations as to why a case went wrong.

programme.title: Wild Kratts
programme.desc: While snowboarding across the Arctic tundra, the Kratt brothers discover a mother polar bear and her young cub who are enemies, but when Zach kidnaps their calf and cub they all come to the rescue.

programme.title: Weather Hunters
programme.desc: It's an exciting day for Lily and her family when they move into their new house.

programme.title: Lyla in the Loop

programme.desc: Lyla and Everett make pins for New Year's Eve; Lyla and Everett clash while making a fun unboxing video for Phan Hardware's Summer Sale.

programme.title: Carl the Collector
